evidence_items.title: Melissa refuses schedule change, later accepts; mentions Kyra discomfort and custody concerns
evidence_items.description: Melissa initially refused to honor a pre-agreed exchange plan, citing work demands, then agreed to reschedule for the following evening. More significantly, Melissa's later message explicitly references Kyra's discomfort as a factor in family conflict and frames Eli's custody petition as opportunistic, suggesting she is aware custody decisions may hinge on household stability and conflict levels. This indicates Melissa may be managing household dynamics (and Kyra's emotional state) with Eli's custody case in mind.

evidence_items.title: Melissa repeatedly violates boundary conditions, escalates emotional pressure after Eli reverses furniture storage agreement
evidence_items.description: Melissa agreed to Eli's boundary condition (use friends, not mutual family/ex's family for moving help), then immediately and repeatedly violated it by asking Josh (Eli's family) to help. When Eli reversed his storage agreement due to ignored conditions, Melissa responded with sustained emotional escalation, accusatory language ("bully," "mean," "cruel," "hateful"), and pressure tactics characterizing a reasonable boundary as an attack on her ability to parent. She ignored Eli's request to stop texting while working and made religious/moral judgments ("God is watching").

evidence_items.title: Melissa accuses Eli of abuse, makes veiled alienation statement, seeks distance
evidence_items.description: Melissa makes an unsubstantiated claim of abuse occurring 'behind the scenes' without specifics, then paradoxically asserts she shields Kyra from this alleged abuse to preserve the father-daughter relationship. She simultaneously attempts to limit Eli's contact time with Kyra by requesting he give 'it a break' and 'leave us alone for the weekend,' suggesting gatekeeping of access. The accusation lacks any supporting detail and appears retaliatory to a reasonable parenting boundary (limiting unnecessary details shared with the child about logistics).

evidence_items.title: Melissa withholds concert access to coerce location tracking; uses child as leverage
evidence_items.description: Melissa explicitly threatens to deny Kaden concert access unless she receives location tracking information, framing this as conditional punishment. She also disputes Eli's account of a conversation with Kyra, and Eli references a prior court order instructing both parents not to involve the child in adult disputes. This demonstrates gatekeeping of privileges contingent on compliance with parental demands and suggests a pattern of involving children in adult conflicts.

evidence_items.title: Melissa allegedly told Kyra that Eli brainwashes her and manipulates people
evidence_items.description: Eli confronts Melissa via text about alleged statements she made to Kyra characterizing him as manipulative, a brainwasher, and deliberately provocative toward Melissa. If accurate, these statements constitute direct alienation messaging to the child, attributing negative motives to the father and framing his parenting as psychologically harmful. The allegations reflect a pattern of negative characterization designed to undermine Kyra's relationship with her father.

evidence_items.title: Melissa accuses Eli of manipulation and gaslighting; defends statements to Kyra

Melissa claims her own statements to Kyra are factual and defensive rather than manipulative, but the message pattern shows she is aware of influencing Kyra's perception of Eli and is now attempting to counter-influence. This demonstrates awareness of the alienation dynamic and defensive justification of statements made to the child.

evidence_items.title: Melissa rejects Eli's relocation plan, frames move as selfish choice, gatekeeps daughter's autonomy
evidence_items.description: Melissa explicitly rejects Kyra's stated preference to relocate with her father, declaring the move 'should not be an option period' and comparing it to unreasonable requests like moving to San Francisco. She frames the relocation as Eli's selfish personal choice rather than considering Kyra's documented wishes. Melissa also makes an accusation that Eli is 'punishing' her by denying furniture storage, which Eli denies and clarifies is a logistics issue. Eli demonstrates reasonable cooperation by affirming Kyra's voice while explaining his reasoning and offering non-controversial help.

evidence_items.title: Melissa refuses to approve court-ordered motion; disputes legal process
evidence_items.description: Melissa refuses to engage with the legal modification process after a judge denied the previous motion, repeatedly stating she will not approve the rewritten agreement Kelly is preparing. She dismisses the court's authority, insists the transaction can proceed on the original denied terms, and becomes increasingly hostile and dismissive of Eli's reasonable attempts to clarify the legal requirements. This represents obstruction of a court-ordered process and refusal to cooperate on material legal matters.

evidence_items.description: Melissa uses threats of relocation (move to Duncan if Eli wins), references to negative letters about him, and explicit financial/custodial leverage language in response to Eli's civil custody proposal. She then insults Eli directly and disputes a lawful custody schedule that follows the parenting plan. The pattern shows emotional coercion, veiled alienation rhetoric regarding 'robbed' years with Kyra, and hostile communication despite Eli's attempts at civil exchange.

evidence_items.title: Eli clarifies fireworks incident; requests accurate context from Melissa
evidence_items.description: Eli received incomplete or misleading information about a fireworks incident and took initiative to investigate directly with the child involved. He clarified that no actual fireworks were lit in the house—only a single firecracker prank by a third party months earlier. Eli requests Melissa provide full context in future communications to avoid mischaracterizations, suggesting a pattern of incomplete information being passed to him.
evidence_items.raw_text: [01:06 PM] Eli Burrup: Discussed "fireworks" with Kaden. He said Phoenix (who is no longer in the band) pranked Kaden by popping a single black cat one time, nearly 5 months ago. Kaden did not light firework OR fireworks in the house. Please provide full context going forward to prevent potential mischaracterizations. I pictured lots of fireworks and Roman candles.

evidence_items.title: Melissa denies Kyra access to grandfather, uses hostile language, makes threats
evidence_items.description: Melissa explicitly refused Kyra's request to visit her grandfather, citing Kyra's headache and emotional state, while simultaneously denying the child relief from the situation. The refusal is paired with escalating hostile communication including name-calling, accusations that Eli is committing 'child abuse' and 'poisoning' the family, and a threat to confiscate the children's phones unless Eli grants her unilateral monitoring access. Melissa also suggests the children are 'regurgitating' Eli's words, indicating a pattern of characterizing the child's expressed preferences as coached rather than genuine.

She uses guilt-based language ('I grew these kids in my BODY'), minimizes Eli's parental role, and positions herself as the victim while blaming Eli for children's negative feelings toward her. This demonstrates clear alienating behavior, emotional manipulation of the child, and gatekeeping of communication devices.

Eli documents his reasoning with reference to professional guidance and maintains clear boundaries.

evidence_items.title: Melissa escalates financial/custody grievances into late-night hostile messages blaming Eli for parental alienation
evidence_items.description: Over a 4+ hour period starting at 12:38 AM, Melissa sends approximately 30+ escalating messages to Eli, shifting from property/financial disputes to accusations of parental sabotage, manipulation, and psychological harm. She uses extreme language (calling him 'piece of shit,' 'terrible person'), threatens to send messages to children about Eli's alleged misconduct, claims he is deliberately creating wedges between her and the children, and frames his parenting decisions as intentional sabotage. She also appears to involve minor children in group text discussions about finances, custody, and parental conflict—the exact behavior Eli explicitly asked her to stop. This pattern shows sustained hostile communication, attribution of malicious intent, and attempts to damage Eli's relationship with the children through inflammatory language and allegations.

Melissa's pattern shows refusal to accept parenting time limits and use of inflammatory language when requests are declined.

evidence_items.title: Melissa yelled at Kyra, accused Eli of brainwashing, escalated in front of child
evidence_items.description: Kyra contacted her father three times in one day seeking emotional support and relief from being yelled at in her mother's home. Melissa directly told Kyra that Eli is "brainwashing" her and intentionally causing her stress—a classic parental alienation statement that blames the father for the child's distress. When Erin (present with Eli) tried to de-escalate by phone, Melissa escalated further by arguing in front of Kyra despite requests to speak privately, maintaining a raised emotional tone and refusing to allow adult conversation. This incident demonstrates sustained alienating language directed at Kyra, emotional dysregulation in front of the child, and hostility toward Eli's support network.
evidence_items.raw_text: .Incident SummaryChild: Kyra Parents: Eli and Melissa Witnesses: Erin (present with Eli), Kyra (directly involved), Your father (transported Kyra)1. Incoming Calls From KyraKyra called three separate times throughout the day asking for support and a break due to being yelled at in her mother's home.She reported that Melissa told her that I (Eli) am “brainwashing her” and that I was “intentionally stressing her out so she would be mean to Kyra during her custody time.”I contacted Melissa to verify whether this was a misunderstanding.Melissa confirmed she made that statement and affirmed that she stood by it.2. Phone Call With ErinDuring the third call, Kyra was speaking with Erin for emotional support.While Erin was speaking to Kyra, Melissa entered the room and initiated an argument in front of Kyra.Erin asked Kyra to step out of the room to reduce escalation and to allow the adults to talk.Erin then attempted to communicate calmly with Melissa, but Melissa continued speaking over Erin and did not allow Erin to respond, maintaining a raised and emotional tone.3. De Escalation AttemptWe chose to allow Melissa to continue speaking without interruption with the intention of allowing her to vent to us to reduce further confrontation around Kyra.After approximately one hour, my father arrived and picked up Kyra to remove her from the situation and ensure she had a calm environment.After Kyra had left the immediate situation, we ended the call.Outcome / Current StatusKyra requested support multiple times due to feeling distressed in her mother’s home.Melissa acknowledged telling Kyra that I am “brainwashing” her.Conflict escalated in front of Kyra, despite attempts to reduce participation and redirect to private conversation.Kyra was removed from the environment and is currently in a calm and regulated setting.

evidence_items.title: Melissa escalates educational decision into personal argument with raised voice and name-calling during call with Eli and Kyra
evidence_items.description: During a call to resolve Kyra's request for online schooling, Melissa initially deferred a decision, then refused the request without discussion. When Eli informed Kyra of the denial, Melissa escalated the conversation into personal conflict involving marital disputes and custody matters, raising her voice and engaging in name-calling while Kyra was present. Eli removed Kyra from the conflict. This incident demonstrates both gatekeeping behavior (unilateral educational decision-making) and verbal abuse in front of the child, causing her documented distress.
evidence_items.raw_text: Date of Incident: November 13, 2025On November 13th, I was on a phone call with my daughter, Kira, to follow up on a matter initially raised on November 11th. Two days prior, Kira had asked her mother, Melissa, whether she could participate in online schooling for the next semester. Melissa had indicated she would consider the request and give an answer later.On the 13th, we contacted Melissa again to obtain a decision. Melissa stated that she still did not have an answer and then, without further discussion, ultimately refused the request outright. I informed Kira that the answer was no, which understandably caused her some distress.At this point, Melissa began to escalate the conversation into a personal argument, including revisiting past marital disputes and introducing topics related to the ongoing custody matter. She raised her voice, engaged in name-calling, and became increasingly agitated.In order to shield Kyra from the conflict, I asked her to leave the room. Melissa continued to vent for over 10 minutes before hanging up. I inquired if she had stopped any medications recently and she refused to answer. Not an admission of guilt but this question arose because of a noticeable pattern of heightened agitation and an inability to engage in calm discussion, which seemed more pronounced than usual.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa began to escalate the conversation into a personal argument, including revisiting past marital disputes and introducing topics related to the ongoing custody matter", "She raised her voice, engaged in name-calling, and became increasingly agitated", "Melissa continued to vent for over 10 minutes before hanging up", "which caused her some distress"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa refuses medical/therapy info, name-calls Eli, schedules therapy during his custody time
evidence_items.description: Eli initiated a reasonable conversation about Kyra's schooling options and requested standard parental information about Kyra's therapy and orthodontist appointments. Melissa refused to provide appointment times, citing boundary violations, and unilaterally scheduled a therapy session during Eli's custody time without notice or coordination. When Eli offered a reasonable accommodation (bringing Kyra himself, arranging for Melissa to meet them), Melissa responded with name-calling and accusations rather than collaborative problem-solving. This pattern demonstrates gatekeeping of medical information, schedule interference, and hostile communication.
evidence_items.raw_text: Date of Incident: November 14, 2025I initiated a conversation with Melissa to discuss Kyra’s feelings about the possibility of online schooling. I suggested that Melissa share her concerns with Kyra directly so they could potentially problem-solve together, rather than rejecting the idea outright.Unfortunately, the conversation quickly escalated. Melissa responded with name calling and accusations rather than addressing the topic constructively.In the same conversation I also asked Melissa for information about Kyra’s upcoming therapy and orthodontist appointments. Melissa refused to provide these details, stating that answering those questions would violate her boundaries and she declined to share the appointment times.Later in the conversation, I discovered that Melissa had scheduled a therapy session for Kyra during my custody time, without informing me. I told Melissa that I would bring Kyra to the appointment myself and that we could arrange for her to meet Kyra there. This led to further accusations and name calling from Melissa.This summary is intended to accompany related text messages documenting the incident and to provide a clear record of events.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa refused to provide these details, stating that answering those questions would violate her boundaries and she declined to share the appointment times", "Melissa had scheduled a therapy session for Kyra during my custody time, without informing me", "the conversation quickly escalated. Melissa responded with name calling and accusations rather than addressing the topic constructively", "This led to further accusations and name calling from Melissa"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa conditions online schooling on control; cites unfounded kidnapping fears
evidence_items.description: Melissa is using her veto power over a reasonable educational request as leverage tied to Kyra's stated preference to relocate to live with Eli. She has imposed a control condition (Melissa as 'primary overseer') and invoked an unfounded accusation of kidnapping potential to justify withholding approval of online schooling, despite Kyra's documented history with virtual/homeschool formats ages 5-11. This gatekeeping decision appears motivated by resistance to Kyra's stated wishes rather than educational concerns.
evidence_items.raw_text: On November 14th, it became apparent through Melissa’s statements that her decision making process regarding Kyra’s request for online schooling was influenced by Kyra’s broader relocation request to Duncan.Melissa stated that she would not consider allowing Kyra to participate in online school unless she (Melissa) be the primary person overseeing it. She also stated that online schooling would make it easier for me to “kidnap” Kyra, and for that reason, she would not allow it.Note: Kyra participated in homeschool and virtual schooling from ages 5 through 11. Her current request for online school is consistent with her prior educational history.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["she would not consider allowing Kyra to participate in online school unless she (Melissa) be the primary person overseeing it", "online schooling would make it easier for me to \"kidnap\" Kyra, and for that reason, she would not allow it", "Kyra's request for online school is consistent with her prior educational history"]

evidence_items.title: Eli's documented response to Kyra's distress over legal/financial matters
evidence_items.description: Eli's journal entry documents his response when Kyra became distressed about legal and financial aspects of the custody case. Eli reassured Kyra that she is not responsible for adult matters, avoided criticizing her mother, and offered emotional support. This demonstrates appropriate parental boundary-setting and child-centered coping strategies in response to what appears to be Kyra's anxiety about the proceedings.
evidence_items.raw_text: Continued from November 22nd:My ResponseI reassured Kyra that:She is not responsible for any adult legal or financial matters.The custody issues are for the adults to handle.She should not worry about money or lawyers.She is loved and none of this is her fault.She can always talk to me if she's upset. I did not make negative comments about her mother. I focused entirely on comforting and reassuring Kyra.Concerns:This incident raises significant concerns about:Age-inappropriate discussions of adult legal and financial matters with the child.Emotional manipulation by discussing costs and suggesting the child is causing financial harm.Yelling at the child during discussions about custody.Placing the child in the middle of parental conflict.Potential pattern of similar incidents (child mentioned previous yelling when friend visited)Action Taken:Provided emotional support to child.Documented incident immediately.Will monitor for similar incidents.Will raise at mediation if pattern continues.May seek counseling support for child if distress continues.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["She is not responsible for any adult legal or financial matters", "The custody issues are for the adults to handle", "She should not worry about money or lawyers", "She is loved and none of this is her fault", "She can always talk to me if she's upset", "I did not make negative comments about her mother"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa discusses $14k legal fees with Kyra, yelling; implies Kyra costs father money
evidence_items.description: Melissa deliberately involved Kyra in financial details of the custody litigation, quantified legal expenses to the child, and directly suggested to Kyra that her father will incur significant costs as a result of her custody preference. This occurred while Melissa was yelling. Kyra called her father crying and distressed, reporting she felt responsible for the financial burden. This conduct places the child in an uncomfortable position regarding her own expressed preferences and creates guilt and anxiety around the custody process.
evidence_items.raw_text: On November 22, 2025, at approximately 1:30 PM, Kyra called me crying and visibly upset. She reported that she and her mother had been getting along well until her mother asked if she wanted to have a friend over. Kyra declined, explaining to her mother that the last time she had a friend visit, her mother felt ignored and began yelling at her.What the Child Reported:Following Kyra's explanation, her mother initiated a conversation about the custody case and associated legal expenses. According to Kyra, her mother:Told her she has already spent $14,000 on legal fees.She asked Kyra if she knew how much that was and stated that "$14,000 is more than I paid for my car".She then told Kyra that I (her father) mentioned I was probably going to get a lawyer, and stated to Kyra: "and now you're going to cost your dad a bunch of money too"She was yelling during this conversationChild's Emotional State:Kyra was crying when she called me. She sounded distressed, anxious, and upset. She appeared to feel responsible for the financial burdens being discussed by her mother.What I Heard: While on the phone with Kyra, I could hear her mother in the background. Her mother was speaking loudly and appeared to be accusing me and my wife of talking negatively about her to Kyra during our phone conversations.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Told her she has already spent $14,000 on legal fees", "$14,000 is more than I paid for my car", "and now you're going to cost your dad a bunch of money too", "She was yelling during this conversation", "Kyra was crying when she called me. She sounded distressed, anxious, and upset. She appeared to feel responsible for the financial burdens being discussed by her mother."]

evidence_items.title: Melissa undermines Eli's parenting authority; allows Kaden to circumvent consequences during Eli's time
evidence_items.description: Eli set a consequence (girlfriend restriction during his parenting time). Melissa actively facilitated Kaden spending daytime hours at her home during Eli's scheduled time to circumvent this consequence, demonstrating a pattern of not supporting Eli's parental authority. When Eli attempted calm discussion, Melissa raised her voice and terminated the call. This shows gatekeeping of parenting time and undermining of Eli's disciplinary decisions.
evidence_items.raw_text: On November 26, 2025, during a group call with Melissa and Kaden, Melissa made several accusations about me in front of Kaden, stating that I was responsible for the problems at her home. She said she needed my help enforcing consequences because Kaden was refusing to listen to her.Melissa stated that the consequence she wanted was for Kaden to give her his keys and phone, clean the house and her car, and remain at her home during my scheduled parenting time until the cleaning was finished. I told her she could ground him during her parenting time, but that once it was time for him to be at my home, he needed to follow the parenting schedule. I told Kaden he needed to listen to his mother and give her his keys. He said he was refusing. I informed him that if he left her house without permission, he was to come directly to my home, hand over his keys and phone, and he would be grounded for the duration of her parenting time.Previously, I had told Kaden that his girlfriend was no longer allowed at my home during my parenting time as a consequence. In response, Kaden arranged to spend daytime hours at Melissa’s home during my parenting time so his girlfriend could visit there instead. Melissa agreed to this arrangement, which had the effect of allowing Kaden to avoid the consequence I had set in my home. When I attempted to call Melissa back to discuss the situation calmly, she raised her voice and ended the call.This entry reflects my efforts to support Melissa’s authority and consequences in her home, while noting that she has not supported or enforced my parenting decisions and consequences during my parenting time.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa made several accusations about me in front of Kaden, stating that I was responsible for the problems at her home", "Melissa agreed to this arrangement, which had the effect of allowing Kaden to avoid the consequence I had set in my home", "she has not supported or enforced my parenting decisions and consequences during my parenting time", "When I attempted to call Melissa back to discuss the situation calmly, she raised her voice and ended the call"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa refused scheduled drop-off, violated parenting plan transportation obligation
evidence_items.description: On the scheduled drop-off date (November 26), Melissa refused to transport Kyra per the parenting plan, instead demanding Eli pick her up despite the written order assigning drop-off responsibility to the relinquishing parent. This represents a pattern violation (stated as recurring). Melissa later completed the transport without notifying Eli, creating logistical confusion and requiring Eli's ill wife to prepare for pickup. The lack of communication about the change demonstrates gatekeeping behavior and failure to cooperate on basic execution of custody exchanges.
evidence_items.raw_text: On November 26, 2025,  today’s scheduled drop-off, Melissa was due to bring Kyra to my home at 5:30 p.m. pursuant to our parenting plan. A few minutes after the scheduled time, I called to confirm her estimated arrival.During the call, Melissa stated that she would not be dropping Kyra off and that it was my responsibility to come pick her up, even though the parenting plan specifies that the parent relinquishing custody is responsible for transportation. This isn't the first time we have had this issue and discussion. After she declined to complete the drop-off, I notified Melissa that Kaden was asleep and unable to pick her up. I informed her that I would send my wife, Erin, even though Erin was ill today.Shortly after leaving, Erin called Kyra to ensure she was packed and ready. During that call, Kyra reported that Melissa was now driving her to my home. We did not receive any communication from Melissa that she had changed plans or that she was en route.Kyra arrived safely.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa stated that she would not be dropping Kyra off and that it was my responsibility to come pick her up, even though the parenting plan specifies that the parent relinquishing custody is responsible for transportation", "This isn't the first time we have had this issue and discussion", "We did not receive any communication from Melissa that she had changed plans or that she was en route"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa and Eli cooperate on Kyra's film project pickup; pleasant interaction
evidence_items.description: Eli documents a cooperative exchange with Melissa where she picked up items for Kyra's extracurricular film project. The interaction was calm, Melissa's demeanor was pleasant, and Eli accommodated her requests without resistance. This demonstrates both parents' willingness to support Kyra's activities and communicate constructively.
evidence_items.raw_text: November 25, 2025, on Tuesday at approximately 4:45pm, Melissa came over with Kyra to pick up items related to a short film project Kyra is involved in. The visit was calm and productive. Melissa briefly walked through the house and pointed out several items she would like me to leave behind when I move. I agreed to leave all the items she requested without issue. Her general demeanor was pleasant and relatively calm. Overall, the interaction was positive, cooperative, and marked by minimal contention. I am noting this interaction to document constructive communication and shared support for Kyra’s extracurricular activities.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["The visit was calm and productive.", "Her general demeanor was pleasant and relatively calm.", "Overall, the interaction was positive, cooperative, and marked by minimal contention.", "I agreed to leave all the items she requested without issue."]

evidence_items.title: Melissa's 45-min hostile call, yelling accusations, then group-text alienation of Kaden
evidence_items.description: Melissa initiated a 45-minute call with hostile, accusatory language and yelling about a parenting boundary issue. After Eli set appropriate limits, Melissa escalated by adding Kaden to a co-parenting app group text where she made financially distressing statements, referenced embarrassing legal content in front of the child, made false claims about Eli's care and priorities, and directly contradicted Eli's documented instruction to the child. This pattern demonstrates active alienation through guilt induction, false narratives about paternal abandonment, and involving the minor in adult legal/financial disputes.
evidence_items.raw_text: November 25,2025, at approximately 6:40 pm, I received a phone call from Melissa asking whether I had given Kaden permission to engage in a specific activity. I explained that I had not authorized it and restated the boundaries I had previously communicated to Kaden. I expressed support for her enforcing consistent expectations at her home and explained how I would handle the situation during my parenting time.The call lasted approximately 45 minutes and was marked by Melissa yelling, making multiple inaccurate accusations, and insisting I was responsible for the situation and needed to “fix it.” I found the escalation confusing and difficult to reconcile with the constructive interaction we had earlier that same day. Based on her demeanor, I asked whether she had been drinking or using substances; she denied it.Following the call, Melissa added Kaden to a group text within the co-parenting app. While I was unable to view Kaden’s responses, Melissa made several inappropriate and inflammatory remarks in the thread, including:Stating she had spent $18,000 and couldn't pay for fines;Claiming she didn’t want Kaden “in the system” any more than what I had already caused through his affidavit;Quoting or referencing an embarrassing part of the affidavit in front of him to undermine my parental authority;Claiming I had a “new wife and a new life” and I don't care much about him and that only she cared about him;Attempting to convince Kaden that I had given him permission for the behavior in question, even though we both stated I had not.I responded in the thread by reminding Melissa not to involve Kaden in legal or financial discussions. She told me not to tell her what to do. All messages are saved in the co-parenting app and can be referenced if necessary.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["marked by Melissa yelling, making multiple inaccurate accusations, and insisting I was responsible for the situation and needed to 'fix it.'", "Claiming she didn't want Kaden 'in the system' any more than what I had already caused through his affidavit", "Claiming I had a 'new wife and a new life' and I don't care much about him and that only she cared about him", "Attempting to convince Kaden that I had given him permission for the behavior in question, even though we both stated I had not", "She told me not to tell her what to do"]

evidence_items.title: Journal entry 11/29/2025
evidence_items.raw_text: November 27–28, 2025For Thanksgiving 2025, I had the Thanksgiving holiday with the children pursuant to our custody order. On Thanksgiving Day, during my custody time, I encouraged both children to spend some time with Melissa. Kaden refused, but Kyra agreed, saying, “I guess it’s the right thing to do.” After we finished our family Thanksgiving, Kyra called Melissa and asked if she would like to go see a movie and spend time together.Melissa stated she was tired but asked if they could go the next day instead. Kyra agreed to that plan and then asked me if she could spend time with Melissa the following day, during my custody time. I agreed.On November 28, 2025, Kyra was picked up at roughly 2:00 p.m. and dropped off around 5:30 p.m. This entry reflects that I had the Thanksgiving holiday with the children and that I was flexible with my custody time to encourage and support their relationship with their mother.

evidence_items.title: Melissa refuses online school proposal and educational opportunity for Kyra; inconsistent position vs. Kaden enrollment
evidence_items.description: Eli proposed Kyra enroll in an online school program she was accepted to, with a temporary schedule adjustment to address Melissa's stated concern about household conflict between her and Kaden. Melissa refused to consider the proposal, applying a different standard than she used for Kaden's online enrollment, preemptively framed Eli's proposals as custody-motivated, and terminated discussion before the full proposal was presented. This demonstrates gatekeeping of educational opportunity and preemptive dismissal of reasonable co-parenting dialogue.
evidence_items.raw_text: On December 2, 2025, I called Melissa and then attempted to revisit our prior discussions about Kyra attending online school again. I informed her that Kyra has been accepted to Union's online program for next semester and explained that online school could be beneficial for both children and could support a temporary adjustment to our current 50/50 weekly custody schedule. I proposed a temporary arrangement in which one parent would have Kyra for one week while the other parent has Kaden, and the children would then switch households the following week, so that each child could have greater privacy and personal space. I also stated that the only residence I was able to lease within the school district on short notice is a two‑bedroom, two‑bathroom apartment. In response, Melissa stated that if I needed more space, I should simply remain in the marital residence, despite the fact that we both signed a court‑ordered agreement for her to take over the residence.Melissa stated that she would not agree to Kyra attending online school, explaining that she is not home during the day to supervise schooling and that she does not believe it is fair that Kyra would be with me during daytime hours while she would only see her in the evenings. She previously agreed to and participated in enrolling Kaden in online school under similar circumstances. In prior conversations, Melissa has also stated that having both children in her home at the same time is difficult because she and Kaden frequently fight, and that this makes the environment uncomfortable for Kyra. The proposed temporary weekly split‑child schedule was intended in part to address that concern; however, before I was able to present that portion of the proposal, Melissa stopped the discussion and stated that she would not negotiate any of the topics raised. She further expressed that she believes any proposal I make is part of an attempt to take Kyra away from her.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa stated that she would not agree to Kyra attending online school, explaining that she is not home during the day to supervise schooling and that she does not believe it is fair that Kyra would be with me during daytime hours while she would only see her in the evenings.", "She previously agreed to and participated in enrolling Kaden in online school under similar circumstances.", "before I was able to present that portion of the proposal, Melissa stopped the discussion and stated that she would not negotiate any of the topics raised.", "Melissa has also stated that having both children in her home at the same time is difficult because she and Kaden frequently fight, and that this makes the environment uncomfortable for Kyra.", "she believes any proposal I make is part of an attempt to take Kyra away from her"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa raises voice, calls Eli 'horrible person' during Christmas planning call
evidence_items.description: During a December 3 phone call regarding Christmas arrangements, Melissa escalated to raised voice and name-calling ('horrible person'), blamed Eli for 'current circumstances,' and rejected his proposal for separate celebrations based on both children's expressed preferences. Eli attempted rational discussion about reducing household conflict through online school and schedule adjustments, but Melissa dismissed the proposals and pivoted to financial accusations rather than engaging substantively on parenting needs.
evidence_items.raw_text: On December 3, 2025, Melissa called me upset after I informed her that we would not all be getting together on Christmas because I did not believe it was in the children’s best interest. Both children have expressed that a combined Christmas would feel awkward, and they preferred separate celebrations.During the call, Melissa raised her voice and stated that I was “taking everything away from her” and that I caused the current circumstances. I told her I am not willing to participate in joint family gatherings when we have been unable to have productive conversations regarding the children’s needs.I attempted to explain again how online school for Kyra and a temporary weekly split-child schedule could reduce conflict in her home and benefit both children. Melissa did not acknowledge this and instead told me I was a “horrible person” for not agreeing to have Christmas at her house with my family.I clarified that my parents might choose to visit her home separately for their traditional pancake breakfast, but that my household—myself, my wife, the children, my parents, and my brother and his wife—would be celebrating Christmas together on December 27.Melissa then stated that I was costing her $27,000 in the divorce. I reminded her that I have repeatedly attempted to resolve matters outside of court, but she has consistently refused to negotiate even basic parenting issues, often dismissing any proposal I make based on her belief that it is intended to disadvantage her.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa raised her voice and stated that I was 'taking everything away from her'", "told me I was a 'horrible person' for not agreeing to have Christmas at her house", "Melissa then stated that I was costing her $27,000 in the divorce", "she has consistently refused to negotiate even basic parenting issues"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa allows unexplained school absences, withholds notification, blocks online school to limit Eli contact
evidence_items.description: Melissa is unilaterally authorizing school absences without informing Eli, failing to supervise Kyra during these absences despite claiming productivity, and explicitly rejecting Kyra's request for online school based on reducing Kyra's time with Eli. Melissa's own pattern of calling out of work appears to model avoidance behavior that Kyra is adopting. This represents gatekeeping (blocking online school to control custody time), withholding (not informing Eli of absences), and parental alienation (framing more time with father as negative).
evidence_items.raw_text: December 9, 2025, I have noticed a pattern, based on what Kyra has told me, that when Melissa is home from work during her parenting time and Kyra asks to stay home from school, Melissa often allows her to stay home and calls the school to report her as sick. I am not informed of these decisions and only learn about the absences because Kyra tells me afterward.Last night, Kyra called me upset and crying after she again asked Melissa to allow her to move to online school. Kyra said Melissa told her no because it would result in Kyra spending more time with me than with Melissa. Kyra also told me that Melissa has been calling out of work and said she is probably going to “get fired,” which may be signaling to Kyra that it is acceptable for her to take similar “mental health” days from school.Kyra also disclosed last night that she has a lot of homework she is behind on and that her motivation to complete schoolwork is getting worse because she feels unable to focus in her current school environment.Today, Kyra told me she stayed home from school during Melissa’s time for the same “lock in and focus” reason and because she was not feeling well mentally. I asked Melissa why Kyra stayed home, and she said it was a mental health day and that Kyra was being productive, but she also said she had not checked on Kyra. Later, Kyra told me she had not been productive and had spent the time doomscrolling instead of working on schoolwork.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa told her no because it would result in Kyra spending more time with me than with Melissa", "I am not informed of these decisions and only learn about the absences because Kyra tells me afterward", "she said it was a mental health day and that Kyra was being productive, but she also said she had not checked on Kyra", "Kyra also disclosed last night that Melissa has been calling out of work", "Kyra told me she had not been productive and had spent the time doomscrolling instead of working on schoolwork"]
evidence_items.tags: ["school absences", "unilateral decisions", "custody time control", "alienation motivation", "lack of supervision"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa withholds filming details, extracts promise from Kyra to exclude Eli
evidence_items.description: Melissa refused to provide Elijah with filming location and scheduling details during his scheduled parenting time, preventing his attendance and support. More significantly, Melissa extracted a promise from Kyra to ensure Melissa handled transportation, and when questioned, Kyra disclosed this was not her own preference but a condition imposed by her mother. This demonstrates gatekeeping of activities, withholding of information, and direct emotional manipulation of the child to enforce loyalty to the mother during the father's parenting time.
evidence_items.raw_text: Today, December 9th, Melissa texted me to ask if it would be acceptable for Kyra to finish some filming during my scheduled parenting time this coming weekend, and if so, for what blocks of time. I told her that was not a problem on my end and provided clarification on when I would be available. I also explained that I would handle transportation since it is my parenting time.Melissa responded that she wanted to be the one to handle the transportation. I told her that because it is my parenting time, I would like the location and scheduling information and that she was still welcome to attend if she wanted to be present. Melissa replied that she would instead reschedule the filming for her own parenting time. I asked again for the filming information so I could attend and support Kyra, but Melissa refused and told me I was not welcome on the set.During these conversations, my wife contacted Kyra to ask whether she had a preference regarding who drove her to filming. Kyra stated that Melissa had made her promise that if any filming took place during my parenting time, Kyra would ensure Melissa was the one to take her. When asked whether that was actually her preference, Kyra said, “Well, no, but she made me promise.”As of now, Melissa is still refusing to provide the filming details, which prevents me from being able to contact the director to understand the rules and expectations during filming or to support Kyra appropriately.It is also important to note that this is not the first time Melissa has withheld information regarding Kyra’s extracurricular activities or told me outright that I was not welcome at events connected to supporting our daughter.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa responded that she wanted to be the one to handle the transportation", "Melissa replied that she would instead reschedule the filming for her own parenting time", "Melissa refused and told me I was not welcome on the set", "Melissa had made her promise that if any filming took place during my parenting time, Kyra would ensure Melissa was the one to take her", "Well, no, but she made me promise"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa appeared intoxicated during custodial time; took day off work with child home from school
evidence_items.description: On December 9, during Melissa's custodial time, she took the day off work and kept Kyra home from school. When Eli arrived to drop off keys that evening, Melissa appeared visibly intoxicated with glazed eyes, slow speech, and strong alcohol odor. Kyra reported that Melissa had yelled at her during an argument about Melissa's spending while complaining about money, placing the child in an emotional conflict. The combination of apparent intoxication during full-day custodial responsibility and the documented hostile interaction with Kyra raises concerns about child supervision and emotional safety.
evidence_items.raw_text: December 9, 2025, Melissa took the day off work and Kyra stayed home from school during Melissa’s parenting time. This evening I went to Melissa’s residence to drop off some keys. When I arrived, Melissa was in her bedroom with the door closed, and Kyra let me in. Melissa came out to the doorway. She appeared visibly intoxicated. I could smell a strong odor of alcohol coming from her breath and pores. Her eyes were glazed and squinty, and she was talking very slowly, as if she were working hard to appear sober. I explained which keys were for what, and she then asked me to help her bring up a new vacuum and talked about a new lawn mower she had bought on sale.Kyra recently told me that she and Melissa had gotten into a fight after Melissa purchased new bedroom furniture. According to Kyra, Melissa had been complaining to her about money, and when Kyra asked why she was spending so much on new things, Melissa became upset and yelled at her. Given that Melissa had been home all day while Kyra was also home from school, I am concerned about when she started drinking and what would have happened if there had been an emergency while she was in that condition.

I could smell a strong odor of alcohol coming from her breath and pores. Her eyes were glazed and squinty, and she was talking very slowly, as if she were working hard to appear sober", "Kyra recently told me that she and Melissa had gotten into a fight after Melissa purchased new bedroom furniture", "Melissa had been complaining to her about money, and when Kyra asked why she was spending so much on new things, Melissa became upset and yelled at her", "Given that Melissa had been home all day while Kyra was also home from school, I am concerned about when she started drinking and what would have happened if there had been an emergency while she was in that condition"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa's escalating verbal abuse, police threat, and gatekeeping during custody exchange
evidence_items.description: Melissa initiated three successive phone calls (4:27 PM, 4:32 PM, 5:26 PM) during planned custody exchange, escalating from demands about parenting rules to yelling, name-calling, and threats to call police on Kaden. She cancelled Kyra's planned church activity mid-exchange, then refused Eli's offer to take the children temporarily while she recovered from stress. She subsequently locked the doors and demanded Kaden stay with Eli, citing lawyer instruction against direct contact—contradicting her own immediate text confirmations to him. This pattern demonstrates hostile communication, unilateral gatekeeping of access, and emotional manipulation during a child exchange.
evidence_items.raw_text: December 10, 2025, Melissa had originally agreed to take Kyra (daughter) to church to spend time with her friends. Melissa called me demanding I enforce new rules she'd set during her parenting time regarding Kaden. When I asked why these were suddenly changing (noting past allowances), she became combative, yelling, screaming, calling me names, and blaming me for everything. I explained I prefer logical parenting for a 17-year-old. First call: ~4:27 PM CST, lasted ~4.5 minutes; I ended it as we weren't progressing. She called back ~4:32 PM (~14.5 minutes), upset I was nearby ("too far away to support her," then angry I was in town). She threatened to call police over Kaden's non-compliance and did so. While on the phone with police, Kyra asked Melissa if she would take her to church; Melissa said no, but gave permission for me to take her. By the time I arrived, Kyra late for her friends' church, so we attended our usual church together instead.Third call ~5:26 PM (~3 minutes): Melissa said she had called police, she requested they pull Kaden over, give him a ticket, and possibly revoke/extend his license restrictions. She then tried to rehashed everything, yelling, calling me names, and blaming me. I noted past allowances she gave Kaden with this exact scenario. She said it doesn't matter. After she had repeatedly started she was tired, stressed and overwhelmed I offered to take them temporarily to give her a break. She refused.Eventually, Melissa asked me to take Kaden overnight, claiming I had given permission to disobey (which I hadn't) and stating she was locking the doors. She sent me a text stating her lawyer instructed no direct contact with me, then sent texts confirming doors locked and Kaden to stay with me. I suggested consistent rules/consequences so he doesn't run to my house (or get sent) over disagreements; she responded unconstructively, so I agreed to take Kaden in order to de-escalate. Full audio/texts available/transcript on request.

She refused", "she was locking the doors. She sent me a text stating her lawyer instructed no direct contact with me"]

evidence_items.title: Eli enforces boundaries with son Kaden; documents disciplinary approach and rule-setting
evidence_items.description: Eli documents his parenting approach with his son Kaden following a rule violation. He demonstrates consistent enforcement of household rules, communication of expectations to both the child and co-parent, and conflict resolution through calm conversation rather than escalation. This shows structured parenting, respect for the co-parent's authority, and logical boundary-setting—all positive indicators of Eli's parenting capacity and reasonableness.
evidence_items.raw_text: December 11, 2025 continued:For the record, I did not condone Kaden driving to Oklahoma City and told Melissa that if it had been my parenting time, he would not have gone. During my parenting time, Kaden does not directly violate my rules because we have logical conversations when he disagrees with what I am asking. I do not allow him to disrespect his mother and have made that clear to both of them. When he came home around midnight, we had a calm conversation in which he apologized for everything that happened, acknowledged the boundaries and consequences, apologized for not listening when I told him not to go, and promised to follow the rules and do better going forward.

evidence_items.title: Melissa ignores refund requests and threatens to exclude son from residence
evidence_items.description: Melissa ignored multiple requests to return Venmo payments (approximately $200+) that Eli sent in good faith for bills he later discovered remained in his name, demonstrating communication avoidance and potential financial withholding. Separately, Melissa made conditional statements threatening to exclude Kaden from her home based on behavioral expectations, blamed Eli for Kaden's conduct, and refused to clarify her position on custody time, creating ambiguity. Eli responded reasonably by accommodating Kaden's safety while maintaining Melissa's access rights.
evidence_items.raw_text: December 11, 2025, Melissa received two Venmo payments from me yesterday for what I believed were the final water and gas bills on the former marital home. I later confirmed the water bill is still solely in my name and that I remain responsible for it. I asked Melissa to return the money; she claims she never accepted the payments, but my Venmo records show they were completed and the funds withdrawn from my account. I have asked her a couple of different times to return the funds, and those texts have been ignored.Later that day, Melissa sent multiple texts about Kaden, stating he “will not be allowed to stay with me” if he continues to “blatantly disrespect” her, not clean up after himself, or “be a team player in coexisting.” She said he “crossed the line (that you encouraged)” and that it would be hard to come back from, adding that she will not be “blatantly disrespected like this ever again.” She also wrote that he should be grounded from his phone, car, and friends and accused me of letting him “live it up with no consequences,” calling that “stellar parenting Eli.”I replied, asking, “Are you wanting Kaden to stay with me full time?” Melissa responded, “No I’m saying he has to be respectful,” without giving any clear plan for her court‑ordered time beyond vague statements about respect. When she implies or states that he cannot stay with her, I keep Kaden with me so he has a safe and stable place, while continuing to make him available for her parenting time under the custody order.

evidence_items.raw_text: On December 14, 2025, Kyra had a Christmas special she was performing in at her piano teacher’s church. Earlier in the week I sent the event information and invitation to Melissa so she would be aware of the date, time, and location; I didn’t receive any response to that message. Melissa attended the event. Kyra’s performance went well and the overall atmosphere was pleasant. evidence_items.title: Melissa conditions Kyra's attendance at father's family event on her own invitation, then circumvents boundary by contacting relative directly
evidence_items.description: Melissa conditioned Kyra's attendance at father's family Christmas gathering on Melissa being invited, directly controlling the child's access to paternal family relationships. After Eli set a clear boundary, Melissa circumvented it by contacting Eli's uncle directly to request inclusion. Additionally, Melissa excluded Eli from Kyra's movie filming activity based on alleged discomfort and director disapproval, implying she had spoken negatively about him to third parties. This pattern demonstrates gatekeeping behavior and potential alienation through negative characterization.
evidence_items.raw_text: Later on December 14, 2025, I dealt with a conflict regarding an extended family Christmas gathering that falls on Melissa’s parenting time. Kyra told me she wanted to attend my family’s Christmas event.When Kyra asked Melissa if she could attend, Melissa told her she could only go if Melissa was also allowed to come, effectively conditioning Kyra’s attendance on Melissa being invited. I have made it clear to Melissa on multiple occasions that these family functions are not shared events and that she is not invited.Despite this, Melissa went around me and contacted my uncle directly to ask if she could attend the party. In that conversation, she told him she is still “family” and claimed she has no family of her own. She did this knowing she was not invited and after I had set a clear boundary.This behavior contrasts with Melissa’s actions when Kyra was filming for a movie she is in. During that time, Melissa refused to allow me to come to the set, saying it would be uncomfortable for Kyra and claiming the director, who has never met me, did not like me. When I pointed out that any negative opinion would have had to come from her speaking negatively about me, she did not deny it. (Full audio of this being said while I was attempting to help her with Kaden is available upon request.)Overall, this situation reflects a pattern in which Melissa attempts to insert herself into my family functions by contacting my relatives directly, while simultaneously excluding me from Kyra’s activities and speaking negatively about me to others. I am maintaining the boundary that my family controls its own guest list, that Melissa is not invited to my family Christmas functions, and that whether Kyra attends during Melissa’s parenting time is Melissa’s choice. It is not something that requires Melissa’s inclusion.

When Kyra asked Melissa if she could attend, Melissa told her she could only go if Melissa was also allowed to come", "Melissa went around me and contacted my uncle directly to ask if she could attend the party", "she told him she is still 'family' and claimed she has no family of her own. She did this knowing she was not invited and after I had set a clear boundary", "Melissa refused to allow me to come to the set, saying it would be uncomfortable for Kyra and claiming the director, who has never met me, did not like me", "When I pointed out that any negative opinion would have had to come from her speaking negatively about me, she did not deny it"]

evidence_items.title: Eli's documented attempts to resolve utilities; Melissa shifts to unrelated property issues
evidence_items.description: Eli initiated good-faith communication about a shared financial obligation (past-due utility bill), provided context about his prior payment and lack of access post-move, and requested specific documentation needed to settle any prorated amounts. Melissa acknowledged the issue but did not respond substantively to his settlement proposal. Instead, she shifted the conversation to unrelated property matters (alleged leak, trash) that appear designed to deflect or create counter-grievances rather than resolve the utility issue cooperatively.
evidence_items.raw_text: December 17, 2025, at approximately 10:21 a.m., I contacted Melissa to inform her that the gas bill for the former marital residence was past due. The account was primarily in her name, and as of my last day in the home (December 8, 2025), I didn't have the ability to disconnect or manage that service. Prior to moving out, when I received the bill, I paid the balance due via Venmo with the understanding that she would handle the account going forward.Melissa responded that my name was not on the gas bill and stated that she intended to take care of it. She indicated that she had attempted to set up automatic withdrawal, but that it would not take effect until the following month and that she had not realized this.At approximately 10:51 a.m., I informed Melissa that I no longer have access to the utility accounts or billing statements. I advised that if any prorated amount was owed for the period prior to my move out date of December 8, she would need to provide a copy of the prorated bill so that the amount could be settled, unless she intended to assume responsibility for the utilities moving forward.Melissa did not respond directly to that message. Instead, she asked about an alleged leak in the downstairs half‑bathroom. I replied that the half‑bathroom was in working order and that there was no leak present at the time I moved out. When she continued to press the issue, I did not respond further.Melissa also asked about trash located on the side of the house. I did not respond to that inquiry. The home was purchased “as is.” I had intended to remove the items as a courtesy, but I ran out of time and no longer had the ability to do so prior to vacating the property.Additionally, although not required by the final order, I paid to have the home professionally cleaned prior to vacating the property as a courtesy to Melissa.

Instead, she asked about an alleged leak in the downstairs half\u2011bathroom", "I had intended to remove the items as a courtesy, but I ran out of time and no longer had the ability to do so prior to vacating the property"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa withholds appointment details, attempts unilateral school removal during Eli's parenting time
evidence_items.description: Melissa failed to provide Eli with orthodontist appointment details despite his request, told him she would reschedule to her parenting week if problematic, then proceeded to attempt removal of Kyra from school during Eli's parenting time without his consent or Kyra's prior knowledge. Pattern shows Melissa controlling medical appointments, excluding Eli from decisions, and acting unilaterally despite earlier representations.
evidence_items.raw_text: December 17, 2025At approximately 12:13 p.m., Melissa messaged that she planned to pick Kyra up from school during my parenting time to take her to an orthodontist appointment and then to lunch. I replied that I did not authorize her to pick Kyra up during my time. Previously, when I asked for Kyra’s upcoming orthodontist and therapy dates and times, Melissa said the next orthodontist appointment was on December 17 and that if this was a problem she would move it to her parenting week. I requested the appointment time; she did not provide it or obtain my agreement to take Kyra during my time, so I understood the appointment would be moved.In my earlier text asking for the appointment time, I stated that I would contact the orthodontist office directly, which I did. On multiple occasions when I have said that I would take Kyra to her appointments, Melissa has become very upset and told me that these appointments are “her thing” and that she is “handling it.” In some instances, she has rescheduled or moved appointments that originally fell during my parenting week so that she could be the one to take Kyra. I have often allowed this to avoid further conflict and stress for Kyra, not because I am unwilling to be involved in her medical and dental care. Because Melissa had said she would move the appointment to her parenting week if it was a problem and then did not provide the time, I reasonably believed it would be moved and did not add it to my calendar.I later learned the appointment was after school hours and that she planned to take Kyra out of school about 30 minutes early during finals week. Kyra did not know about this appointment or about Melissa’s plan to take her out of school early.

evidence_items.title: Eli's clarification of holiday schedule compliance and response to Melissa's accusations
evidence_items.description: Eli documents his review of holiday scheduling disputes initiated by Melissa's accusations of 'stealing' the children from holidays. The entry shows a July 4 good-faith calendar error (Eli believed it was his time due to voluntary courtesy in 2024), Easter weekend cooperation (Eli obtained permission and returned child as agreed), and Labor Day accommodation (Eli agreed to Melissa's request to keep children while she made alternative plans). Eli demonstrates consistent adherence to parenting plan and willingness to accommodate Melissa's requests.
evidence_items.raw_text: July 4, 2025 (Clarification Entry)Melissa accused me of “stealing her kids” on multiple holidays in 2025 and of “grossly icing her out of the family she built over the last 25 years.” In response to these accusations, I reviewed the parenting plan and holiday schedule to understand what she was referring to.Upon review, I realized that July 4, 2025 was scheduled as Melissa’s holiday under the parenting plan. I believed it was my holiday because in 2024, I voluntarily gave Melissa July 4 as a courtesy, even though it was scheduled as my parenting time that year. When July 4, 2025 occurred, Melissa did not reference the parenting plan, request the time, or object, and I reasonably believed it was my scheduled holiday. This was an oversight on both of our parts and not an intentional violation of the parenting plan.The only other situation Melissa appears to be referencing is Easter weekend, which was her holiday in 2025. Prior to that weekend, I asked whether the children could attend a family Easter dinner on Saturday. Melissa agreed to this arrangement. I took Kyra to dinner and returned her afterward. Melissa retained the remainder of the holiday weekend as scheduled.By contrast, Labor Day is Melissa’s assigned holiday every year. For Labor Day 2025, she told me she planned to be out of town, and she chose not to exercise her holiday parenting time. After those plans fell through, she later said she had made “not kid‑friendly” plans for the weekend and asked me to keep the kids from Friday through Monday so she could still have a long holiday weekend. I agreed and adjusted my plans.At no time did I withhold the children, refuse to return them, or act without discussion or agreement. Outside of the July 4 oversight, I have been strictly adhering to the parenting plan’s holiday schedule and will continue to do so.

I believed it was my holiday because in 2024, I voluntarily gave Melissa July 4 as a courtesy.", "I asked whether the children could attend a family Easter dinner on Saturday. Melissa agreed to this arrangement. I took Kyra to dinner and returned her afterward.", "she asked me to keep the kids from Friday through Monday so she could still have a long holiday weekend. I agreed and adjusted my plans.", "At no time did I withhold the children, refuse to return them, or act without discussion or agreement."]

evidence_items.title: Melissa denies Kyra virtual Christmas access despite parenting plan provision
evidence_items.description: Melissa explicitly refused to permit Kyra virtual participation in Eli's family Christmas event, specifically blocking a phone call for the child's stated favorite activity (Dirty Santa exchange). This refusal contradicts the existing parenting plan's provision for liberal telephone visitation. Eli's documented compliance in stopping family contact after learning of harassment allegations demonstrates his adherence to legal guidance, while Melissa's gatekeeping of communication appears retaliatory and contradicts the agreed parenting terms.
evidence_items.raw_text: Christmas Parenting Time Issue Continued:I was subsequently informed by my attorney that Melissa contacted her attorney alleging harassment by me and my family regarding the Christmas gathering and said to leave it alone. I didn't discuss contacting Melissa with any of my family members and wasn't aware any of them intended to contact her. After the call with my attorney, I immediately instructed all family members to cease any communication with Melissa regarding the event.I later let Melissa know Kyra wanted to attend virtually to participate in the family "Dirty Santa" exchange, which Kyra identified as her favorite part of the event. Melissa declined stating "I'm not going to sit here with her on the phone playing dirty Santa at a party that HER family isn't invited to." Per our parenting plan: "THE PARTIES FURTHER AGREE that both parents shall allow liberal telephone visitation."

evidence_items.title: Melissa conditions Christmas attendance on custody swap, calls child 'collateral damage'
evidence_items.description: Melissa conditioned Kyra's attendance at Eli's family Christmas party on Eli surrendering his own scheduled parenting time on December 27. After Eli proposed multiple alternative accommodations and declined the swap, Melissa withheld approval for the party and then told Kyra she was 'collateral damage' and blamed Eli for the outcome. This represents gatekeeping of a meaningful family event, emotional manipulation of the child by framing her exclusion as Eli's fault, and leveraging custody time as a bargaining chip.
evidence_items.raw_text: Christmas Parenting-Time IssueSometime after December 12, 2025, I informed Kyra and Kaden of our extended family's, annual Christmas party scheduled for December 20, 2025, which falls during Melissa's parenting time. I made it clear to the children that any attendance would be subject entirely to Melissa's approval.Kyra contacted Melissa and later reported that Melissa stated the children could attend only if she was also allowed to attend, claiming she was "still family" and that others wanted her there. I reminded her that joint family functions would not occur, as they create tension. Both children have requested that family events remain separate.After being informed she wasn't able to attend, Melissa contacted multiple members of my family requesting an invitation. Kyra reported that Melissa told her she would "think about" allowing them to attend. Kyra sent multiple messages to Melissa over a couple of days seeking clarification, which went unanswered. Melissa later texted me stating she would allow the children to attend on December 20 only if I agreed to substitute December 27, 2025, which Melissa knew was my family's Christmas celebration during my parenting time. This was the only option offered. I proposed multiple alternative accommodations, including partial-day exchanges, all of which were declined.Kyra was visibly upset by the decision not to allow her to attend. She expressed that she looks forward to this party each year. It's the only opportunity she has annually to see many of this portion of her extended family.Kyra later reported that Melissa told her she was "collateral damage" as a result of my refusal to agree to the proposed substitution, and that this outcome was my choice despite Melissa's attempt to allow the children to attend.

evidence_items.title: Melissa conditions party attendance on Eli's schedule concession; uses gift denial as leverage
evidence_items.description: Melissa conditioned Kyra's party attendance on Eli conceding a weekend custody exchange, placing the child in the position of having to persuade her father to grant Melissa's demand. Melissa then told Kyra she would receive no Christmas presents, falsely attributing this to the Branson trip being her gift and making disparaging comments about Eli's family wealth. When confronted, Melissa retracted the statement. Additionally, Melissa requested unilateral restriction of phone contact between Kyra and Eli during Melissa's parenting time, despite Kyra initiating most contact. This pattern uses the child as leverage for custody concessions and attempts to control communication.
evidence_items.raw_text: December 19th continued After arriving at Melissa’s home, Kyra reported that Melissa told her she could attend the party if I gave Melissa the January 9–10 weekend. This contradicted my prior refusal and placed Kyra in the middle by suggesting the decision depended on her persuading me to change my answer.Later that evening, when I dropped off Kyra’s belongings, Kyra reported that she and Melissa had argued. Kyra stated Melissa told her she would not receive any Christmas presents because the Branson trip was her Christmas gift, and commented that Kyra was likely receiving many gifts from her “rich” family. Kyra later reported that Melissa acknowledged this statement was not true and that she had purchased other gifts.Melissa also requested that my wife and I limit phone contact during her parenting time. We rarely initiate calls; Kyra typically calls us. During the drop-off, Melissa was in her room with the door closed, while Kyra was alone in her room.

evidence_items.title: Melissa conditions holiday access on custody schedule swap; reverses prior permission
evidence_items.description: Melissa made a legitimate holiday access request to Eli that appeared time-sensitive (Branson lights), but when Eli offered reasonable accommodation on January 3 with no makeup required, Melissa abandoned that justification and proposed a different trade. One hour before scheduled exchange, Melissa reversed her prior permission for Kyra to attend Eli's family holiday party, conditioning attendance on Eli agreeing to the schedule swap. This demonstrates gatekeeping (withholding access as leverage) and emotional manipulation (using the child's holiday access as a bargaining chip tied to custody time).
evidence_items.raw_text: Journal Entry – December 19, 2025 (Condensed)Today Melissa emailed requesting a holiday schedule change, stating the only workable option was that I keep the children this weekend and she receive the following weekend, which is my designated Christmas parenting time. She claimed this was necessary because it was the last weekend Branson’s holiday lights were operating. I verified that the lights continue the following weekend as well. I let her know that the kids are old enough to decide where to spend their time and if they wanted to go to Branson with her next weekend, I have no problem with that. I let her know that Kyra expressed interest in attending the Branson trip on January 3 during my parenting time if she wished and that I did not require make-up time. After I confirmed January 3 was still within the operating schedule, Melissa abandoned the Branson-based request and instead stated she wanted to accept a prior proposal to trade this weekend for January 9–10, which did not depend on Branson’s schedule. This indicated the request was not contingent on Branson availability.Melissa’s message accepting the January 9–10 trade was sent approximately one hour before the scheduled 5:30 p.m. holiday exchange. I informed her I would drop Kyra off at 5:30 as planned and that schedule changes require at least 24 hours’ notice.Melissa informed both me and Kyra’s therapist, Alice, that Kyra would be allowed to attend my family’s December 20 holiday party with no swap required, stating she wanted Kyra to attend. However, after I declined to exchange weekends, Melissa reversed that position and stated Kyra would not be allowed to attend unless I agreed to the swap.

I verified that the lights continue the following weekend as well", "This indicated the request was not contingent on Branson availability"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa conditions party attendance on parenting time; uses phone control as leverage
evidence_items.description: Melissa conditioned Kyra's attendance at Eli's family Christmas party on transferring his court-ordered parenting time (Jan 9-10), using the child as a negotiating tool. When Kyra reluctantly agreed under pressure, Melissa later attempted to confiscate Kyra's phone to prevent her virtual participation in family activities. Kyra experienced significant emotional distress that night, contacting Eli multiple times in tears, expressing inability to continue this pattern and stating she did not want to remain with Melissa. Eli maintained appropriate boundaries by refusing to negotiate parenting time and providing emotional support.
evidence_items.raw_text: At approximately 11:23 a.m. on December 20, 2025, Melissa called stating she was bringing Kyra to my residence so she could attend my family’s Christmas party. Shortly after Kyra arrived, she told me that Melissa had required her to agree to give her my parenting time on January 9–10 in exchange for being allowed to attend the party.I informed Kyra that she does not have authority to negotiate or modify court-ordered parenting time. I contacted Melissa and advised her that no agreement existed, I was not negotiating parenting time, and that if Kyra’s attendance at the party was conditioned on transferring my court-ordered time, she needed to pick Kyra up and exercise her parenting time. Kyra stated that she did not want to go with Melissa on January 9–10, but felt pressured to agree so she could attend the party.I have consistently allowed Kyra reasonable flexibility to spend time with her mother during my parenting time when Kyra requests it. I do not allow parenting time to be conditioned, leveraged, or negotiated through Kyra, and I communicated this boundary to Melissa.Around 5:15 Melissa told Kyra she didn't care if she went, then told me she couldn't go unless I gave her one of my weekends. I explained that coercion and the use of Kyra to leverage my parenting time is unacceptable.That night, Kyra contacted me multiple times in significant emotional distress. Kyra was crying and stated that Melissa was trying to take her phone so she wouldn't be allowed to participate in the family’s Dirty Santa virtually. Kyra called me from Melissa’s phone several times that night, crying and begging me to help her, stating that she “can’t keep doing this.” I remained on the phone reassuring her that she was loved until Melissa told her to end the call.After the family Christmas party concluded, Kaden went to check on Kyra to ensure she was okay. Kyra told him she didn't think she would be able to sleep and stated that she did not want to stay there anymore.
