evidence_items.title: Melissa accuses Eli of manipulation; escalates to verbal abuse during church; Kyra reports alienation attempt
evidence_items.description: Melissa responded to routine parenting questions by blocking contact and labeling Eli's inquiries as interference. When Eli complied with her demand to cease contact, Melissa initiated a barrage of hostile messages while Eli was at church, including character attacks (evil, sabotage, hiding behind religion) and invoking God's judgment. Most significantly, Kyra reported that Melissa mocked Kyra's religious beliefs, directly attributed those beliefs to Eli's influence in a disparaging way, and made derogatory statements about Eli during a conflict over a family ritual. This demonstrates both direct verbal abuse directed at Eli and coached alienation directed at Kyra by denigrating her autonomous choices and linking them dismissively to her father.
evidence_items.raw_text: On December 21, 2025, I sent Melissa two messages asking whether Kyra would be attending church and how long Kyra's phone restriction would last.Melissa responded that I was interfering with her parenting time, stated that she had not decided about church, and said that I was not needed in those decisions. She asserted that she had given me extra parenting time the day before and claimed that I had misused it.Melissa has alternated between claiming she gifted me hours on the 20th and claiming we traded December 19–20 for January 9–10, despite her having Kyra from the beginning of her parenting time on December 19 until approximately 11:30 a.m. on December 20, which does not support a claim that December 19–20 were 'my' weekends to trade.I replied to confirm that Kyra was grounded from her phone and that I was not welcome to contact her, and I stated that I would cease contact as requested. Melissa then sent a message accusing me of creating drama, manipulating situations, and attempting to trap her into answers. She stated that in the event of an emergency, I could contact Kyra through her.While I was at church, Melissa attempted to call me twice and sent a series of text messages. These messages included accusations that I ruin positive situations, am evil, hide behind religion, and sabotage her. She also referenced unrelated issues, including the house purchase, Kaden's phone use, and alleged holiday interference. One message stated that God was not happy with me and that the children would see the full picture when they are older.Later, Kyra called me clearly upset. Kyra reported that Melissa was attempting to require her participation in a house smudging ritual. Kyra stated that she declined because it conflicted with her religious beliefs. Kyra reported that Melissa made fun of her beliefs and said she only thinks that way because I do and said that I ruin everything. I attempted to calm and comfort Kyra before the call ended.

evidence_items.title: Melissa prevented Kyra from visiting grandparents on Christmas Eve while ill; Kyra isolated and distressed
evidence_items.description: On Christmas Eve, Melissa prohibited Kyra from visiting her grandparents while Melissa was sick, requiring Kyra to remain home. Kyra avoided contact with Melissa due to illness concerns and spent the day alone in her room, becoming emotionally distressed. Kyra repeatedly called her father seeking extended conversation, expressing loneliness and sadness. This incident demonstrates gatekeeping behavior (unilateral prevention of alternative childcare/family contact) combined with resulting emotional impact on the child during a significant holiday.
evidence_items.raw_text: December 24, 2025Kyra reported that Melissa had been sick most of the day and was in her room. Melissa told Kyra she could not go to her grandparents' house and that she had to stay with her. Kyra said she did not want to get sick, so she avoided spending too much face-to-face time with Melissa and ended up spending most of the day in her room alone.My wife called Kyra once to ask her a quick question. Outside of that, Kyra called us multiple times throughout the day. Each time we indicated we needed to end the call, Kyra said she was bored and sad and asked if we could talk to her longer. We spoke with Kyra for extended periods because she repeatedly expressed feeling lonely and isolated while alone in her room on Christmas Eve with Melissa ill and unable to engage with her.

evidence_items.title: Eli accommodates last-minute custody exchange; Melissa provides no clarity or response
evidence_items.description: Eli received an unscheduled custody request from Melissa on Christmas evening (Dec 25) with no specified duration. Eli sought clarification, communicated his availability, and promptly accommodated the request. Melissa did not respond to any of Eli's three messages seeking coordination details or confirming return parameters. This documents Eli's flexibility and good-faith communication efforts contrasted with Melissa's failure to respond or coordinate—a pattern that creates logistical uncertainty and potential scheduling disputes.
evidence_items.raw_text: December 25, 2025At 8:30 p.m., Melissa sent a message stating "Kyra can come over."At 8:32 p.m., I responded: "Sorry your not feeling well, hope you feel better soon. Kyra can come over from when to when?" I did not receive a response with specific times.At 9:35 p.m., I sent a message stating: "We just got on the Creek, headed your way now to pick Kyra up as you requested. Let me know if you need her back before my parenting time starts. Hope you feel better." I did not receive a response.At 9:47 p.m., I sent a message stating: "Pulling in now." I did not receive a response.Kyra was ready and had packed all of her belongings. I picked Kyra up at approximately 9:50 p.m. without any issues. I left it open for Melissa to request Kyra's return at any time during her parenting time before my regular parenting time was scheduled to begin at 5:30 p.m. on December 26. Melissa did not respond.

evidence_items.title: Melissa demands return of 17-year-old Kaden despite his clear refusal and estrangement
evidence_items.description: Melissa is asserting control over 17-year-old Kaden's parenting time despite his explicit refusal to visit and documented estrangement. Her position has shifted inconsistently—first rejecting his presence, then stating he should remain with Eli, now demanding his return. Kaden has demonstrated consistent avoidance on Christmas (refusing gifts and contact). Eli's response demonstrates appropriate parenting: encouraging direct communication between mother and child rather than forcing compliance, and acknowledging the limits of compulsion with a mature teen.
evidence_items.raw_text: On December 27, 2025, Melissa stated that Kaden must return to her home during her parenting time. Kaden is 17 years old, drives independently, and has clearly stated that he does not wish to go to Melissa’s home and is refusing contact at this time.Kaden and Melissa’s relationship has become significantly estranged. On Christmas, Kaden declined gifts from Melissa and refused to visit her, even briefly. To my knowledge, he did not send her any text messages or accept phone calls from her that day.Also, Melissa’s position during her parenting time with Kaden has been inconsistent. She recently stated that she did not want him at her home, then later indicated that it would be best for him to remain with me. Now she has decided that she wants him to return to her home.I have encouraged Kaden to communicate directly with Melissa regarding his concerns. Given his age and independent mobility, I do not have the ability to compel him to attend parenting time. I informed Melissa of this and again encouraged her to address the matter directly with Kaden.

evidence_items.title: Melissa's hostile calls, name-calling, and attempt to involve Kyra in custody dispute over parenting time
evidence_items.description: On December 29-30, 2025, Melissa requested deviation from the parenting schedule for a film production. When Eli declined and offered reasonable alternatives, Melissa escalated by sending harassing texts with name-calling, making multiple hostile phone calls, and directly contacting Kyra to pressure her into convincing Eli to violate the parenting plan. Kyra reportedly told Eli she asked Melissa not to involve her. This pattern demonstrates communication interference, verbal abuse toward Eli, and emotional manipulation by placing Kyra in the middle of a parenting dispute.
evidence_items.raw_text: On December 29, 2025, Melissa requested to have Kyra on January 1st during my parenting time for a few hours to complete final scenes for a movie production. I explained that I would be adhering to our parenting plan 100% both ways, as I had previously communicated. Melissa continued to request this time despite my explanation that we had already made plans. I offered to take Kyra to the production during my next parenting weekend if they needed her at that time, but Melissa declined and continued texting about why she needed to take Kyra herself.On December 30, 2025, Melissa sent multiple texts calling me names and harassing me even after I explained we already had plans. She also called multiple times. When I answered, the conversation did not go well. She wanted to rehash past issues and became hostile, calling me names and hanging up on me. I attempted to explain both over the phone and via text that we had existing plans for January 1st and that I had already spoken with the director to inform him of Kyra's availability during my parenting time.Melissa also contacted Kyra directly and tried to convince her to persuade me to allow Melissa to take her during my parenting time. I asked Melissa not to contact Kyra about my parenting time decisions, as this puts Kyra in the middle of our co-parenting issues. Kyra later told me she had asked Melissa not to involve her and said this matter was between Melissa and me.Melissa was upset and said "this stuff is her lane." She claimed we were slowing down production and making the family look bad. I explained that during my parenting time, I am willing to facilitate Kyra's participation in activities, and that Melissa was welcome to attend as well. However, we would not be altering the parenting schedule after what occurred with the family Christmas party situation. I offered a reasonable alternative for my next weekend, which was rejected.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa sent multiple texts calling me names and harassing me even after I explained we already had plans", "She wanted to rehash past issues and became hostile, calling me names and hanging up on me", "Melissa also contacted Kyra directly and tried to convince her to persuade me to allow Melissa to take her during my parenting time", "I asked Melissa not to contact Kyra about my parenting time decisions, as this puts Kyra in the middle of our co-parenting issues", "Melissa was upset and said \"this stuff is her lane.\""]

evidence_items.title: Melissa accuses Eli of 'holding Kaden hostage,' withholds house access, makes false claims about location tracking
evidence_items.description: Melissa locked Eli out of the house and withheld Kaden's house key, then reversed course after Eli explained the consequences. She made multiple false accusations against Eli (turning off location tracking, giving permission to avoid contact) that Eli documents as untrue. Eli's documented response shows he encouraged Kaden to maintain contact with Melissa despite Kaden's resistance, demonstrating Eli's willingness to support the mother-son relationship rather than alienate.
evidence_items.raw_text: On January 3, 2026, Melissa asked about Kaden's plans for the band room. I informed her Kaden plans to move his belongings out and intended to move everything downstairs on January 4th.Melissa sent multiple texts claiming Kaden went to a concert without her permission and has not contacted her for over a month. She stated she locked the doors and Kaden cannot enter without a key. She accused me of not co-parenting, "holding Kaden hostage," and stated "I am not consenting to my minor son moving out." Melissa had Kyra retrieve Kaden's key on December 20th. I gave Melissa all keys to the house after we closed.She confirmed Kaden could move items downstairs and requested a meeting with him and Miss Alice. She accused me of "weaponizing her parenting" and making it "approved" that Kaden doesn't contact her. I responded that since Kaden can't get into the house, he is no longer planning to move items. She stated she would give him a key.On January 4, 2026, I asked about picking up a dresser. Melissa ignored my question and instead asked about Kaden's band room plans. I stated she should discuss Kaden questions directly with him.Melissa responded claiming I turned off Kaden's location and gave him permission not to share it with her. This is false. I have not turned off his location or given him any permissions. Kaden is 17 and controls his own phone. I have encouraged Kaden to talk to Melissa and explained he needs to allow her to exercise her parenting time, but he has refused and stated he wants nothing to do with her. I cannot compel him to communicate or visit.

This is false. I have not turned off his location or given him any permissions."]

evidence_items.title: Eli provides therapy cost invoice and appointment info; Melissa responds with accusations and communication cutoff
evidence_items.description: Eli initiated contact to fulfill decree obligations by providing Kyra's therapy cost invoice (Melissa's 30% share) and appointment notification. Melissa responded with unspecified accusations described as inappropriate and concerning, then cut off direct communication, instructing Eli to use lawyers. Eli had previously confirmed Melissa's agreement to the therapist change and selected a provider per Kyra's stated preference for a neutral Christian counselor. This incident shows Eli attempting compliance with decree requirements while Melissa imposed attorney-mediated communication, which increases cost and friction.
evidence_items.raw_text: On January 5, 2026, I contacted Melissa to give her the invoice total for her 30% share of out‑of‑pocket costs, as required by the divorce decree, for Kyra’s therapy, and to keep her informed of Kyra’s appointments. After that, she sent a series of messages that were inappropriate and concerning, accused me of things that were inaccurate, and then told me not to talk to her anymore and to go through our lawyers.On December 13, 2025, Melissa agreed to another therapist for Kyra. On December 18, 2025, I told her I had scheduled an appointment for Kyra with Dr. Walker at Christian Family Institute for December 30, 2025, at 4:00 PM. I let her know Dr. Walker is in network and asked for Kyra’s medical insurance through her; she stated she did not have any.This therapist was specifically requested by Kyra, who said she wanted a Christian counselor not associated with Melissa or Alice at Council Oaks. I chose Dr. Walker as a neutral Christian therapist to meet Kyra’s expressed need.I want it clearly understood that I am not contacting attorneys for co‑parenting issues; doing so is not in Kyra’s best interest, for us to spend a lot of money on attorney fees. I will stick strictly to sharing information required by the divorce decree (appointments, invoices) and contacting her only about true child emergencies.

evidence_items.title: Kyra nervous and rushed away when Melissa appeared during stepmother's brief visit
evidence_items.description: Kyra initiated contact with Eli to retrieve her piano book needed for a lesson, and appeared comfortable and engaged in conversation with Eli's wife during a 20-30 minute visit. However, when Melissa appeared, Kyra became visibly nervous and abruptly ended the interaction, suggesting she may have been made to feel uncomfortable or conflicted about contact with her father's household. This pattern of child anxiety upon parental appearance can indicate the child is being coached or made to feel guilt about time spent with the other parent's family.
evidence_items.raw_text: Today, January 6, 2026, at approximately 6:30 p.m., Kyra called me and asked if I could bring her piano book to Melissa’s house because she needed to practice for her piano lesson on Thursday and Melissa did not want to drive the two miles to get it. Because I was not at home, I asked my wife to drop the book off at Melissa’s house. My wife and Kyra sat outside and talked for approximately 20 to 30 minutes about school, homework, and working on getting her room clean. When Melissa came downstairs and walked outside to see what was going on, Kyra looked nervous, said something like “oh, I better go, thank you for dropping my book off,” and ran back inside. There was no argument or conflict, and the purpose of the visit was only to give Kyra her piano book so she could practice.

evidence_items.title: Melissa missed in-person therapy, then participated by phone despite prior insistence on in-person requirement
evidence_items.description: Melissa scheduled joint therapy sessions with Kyra, then withheld session dates/times from Eli, confirmed attendance then failed to appear in person on January 9, 2026, pivoting to phone participation despite having previously argued that in-person attendance was essential. The therapist could not reach Melissa during the session, limiting Kyra's ability to address her concerns. Melissa later told Kyra she was unable to attend in person due to emotional distress, potentially placing her emotional state before her parental obligation to the session Kyra had requested her to attend.
evidence_items.raw_text: At Kyra’s request, I contacted Melissa on Thursday, January 8, 2026, to confirm whether she would attend Kyra’s therapy appointment on Friday, January 9, 2026, at 3:00 p.m., which Kyra specifically asked her to attend because Melissa had missed their prior session. Melissa confirmed approximately two and a half hours before the appointment that she would be there. These Friday therapy appointments were set up and scheduled by Melissa for the specific purpose of working on her relationship and communication with Kyra, and it is of vital importance that Melissa attend them regardless of whose parenting time they fall on; my transporting Kyra does not change that responsibility, especially since Kyra already has a separate, neutral therapist for her individual issues. Melissa has refused to give me the dates and times of these sessions, requiring me to contact the therapist directly to ensure Kyra has transportation and the appointments are properly accounted for. On Friday, January 9, about twenty five minutes into the session, the office was informed that Melissa would not be coming into the office and had requested to participate by phone instead; Kyra later reported that the therapist was unable to reach Melissa for part of the session, so there was only a brief opportunity to address the concerns Kyra had hoped to discuss. Previously, in discussions about Kyra’s possible relocation to Duncan and continuing therapy, Melissa had insisted that therapy had to be in person and that phone based therapy would not be effective, emphasizing the need for both of them to be physically present together, yet she skipped the prior appointment entirely, saying it was too short notice and not calling in despite having the ability to do so, and told Kyra after this most recent appointment that she did not attend in person because she could not stop crying.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["These Friday therapy appointments were set up and scheduled by Melissa for the specific purpose of working on her relationship and communication with Kyra, and it is of vital importance that Melissa attend them regardless of whose parenting time they fall on", "Melissa has refused to give me the dates and times of these sessions, requiring me to contact the therapist directly to ensure Kyra has transportation and the appointments are properly accounted for", "Previously, in discussions about Kyra's possible relocation to Duncan and continuing therapy, Melissa had insisted that therapy had to be in person and that phone based therapy would not be effective, emphasizing the need for both of them to be physically present together", "the therapist was unable to reach Melissa for part of the session, so there was only a brief opportunity to address the concerns Kyra had hoped to discuss", "told Kyra after this most recent appointment that she did not attend in person because she could not stop crying"]

evidence_items.title: Civil exchange during pickup of Kyra's belongings and dresser
evidence_items.description: Eli documented a scheduled exchange where Kyra picked up personal belongings and furniture from Melissa's home. The interaction remained civil and courteous, with no hostile statements or incidents. This represents baseline cooperative co-parenting behavior and provides context that positive exchanges are possible.
evidence_items.raw_text: January 11, 2026 – I took Kyra over to Melissa’s so she could pick up a dresser and some of her belongings she wanted to bring to the apartment. Kayden was already there to have band practice and pack his things. I briefly helped Melissa move an item. The interaction was calm and civil, and Melissa did not say anything negative to me. This was one of the more positive in‑person exchanges we have had recently.

evidence_items.title: Melissa withheld therapy schedule, made unilateral appointment during Eli's parenting time, refused transparency
evidence_items.description: Melissa scheduled a therapy appointment (ultimately found to be under her own name, not Kyra's) during Eli's parenting time without advance notice or discussion, despite his documented prior requests for appointment transparency. When Eli asked to reschedule due to pre-planned birthday events during his parenting time, Melissa refused, claiming no coordination was required and the birthday plans could be easily moved. Melissa then refused to provide a complete therapy schedule despite acknowledging appointments exist under different names, and used accusatory language ('sabotaging,' 'infiltrate') and blame-shifting ('ruin their weekend') regarding Eli's parenting choices and Kyra's autonomous decisions.
evidence_items.raw_text: On January 15, 2026, Melissa texted that she and Kyra had a therapy appointment scheduled for Friday, January 16, at 1:00 PM and asked that I drop Kyra at 12:45 PM, during my parenting time, which runs until 5:30 PM. This appointment had not been shared in advance as required by the parenting plan, despite my prior requests for all of Kyra’s appointment information.I told Melissa that Kyra already had birthday plans scheduled from 11:00 AM to 2:30 PM that day with friends at a venue that could not realistically be rescheduled, and asked that the appointment be moved. Melissa will have Kyra on her actual birthday, so my wife and I planned this party on the only available date during my parenting time. Melissa stated the appointment had been made months earlier, said it did not need to be cleared with me, and claimed the birthday plans could “easily be rescheduled.”Because Melissa had not provided a therapy schedule despite prior requests, I contacted the therapist’s office and was told Kyra did not have an appointment scheduled this week and the referenced appointment was under Melissa’s name, not Kyra’s. I then asked Melissa for a complete list of upcoming therapy appointments, including any booked under her name that Kyra is expected to attend; Melissa acknowledged appointments may be under different names but declined to provide the schedule, calling it “homework.”In related messages, Melissa accused me of “sabotaging” progress, suggested I was not acting in Kyra’s best interest, and said I would “infiltrate” her birthday weekend. She also blamed me for Kyra’s decision to participate in our church fast, saying it would ruin their weekend plans. I have tried to support her choice to fast while encouraging positive time with Melissa. Throughout our communications, I attempted to follow the parenting plan, protect Kyra’s pre‑planned event, and obtain clear information so Kyra’s therapy and her time with both parents can be supported in a stable, child‑focused way.

evidence_items.title: Eli provided notice of therapy, Melissa delayed response then gatekeeping demands
evidence_items.description: Eli provided Melissa with complete written notice of Kyra's therapy appointment 30+ days in advance, including provider details, with Kyra's consent and Melissa's prior agreement to Christian-focused therapy. Melissa did not respond until over a month later, when she suddenly demanded access to the therapist and objected to treatment already underway. Eli's subsequent communication with the provider appropriately protected therapeutic boundaries while allowing reasonable post-session check-ins, demonstrating measured co-parenting judgment.
evidence_items.raw_text: On December 18, 2025, I notified Melissa in writing that Kyra had scheduled her first individual therapy appointment with Dr. Walker at Christian Family Institute for December 30, 2025, at 4:00 PM. I provided full provider and contact information. The therapy was scheduled at Kyra’s request, and Melissa had previously agreed that Kyra could see a Christian-focused therapist. Dr. Walker met that request and was in-network. Melissa did not respond, ask questions, or raise concerns other than stating she wouldn't pay for any of it.January 20, 2026 (over 30 days later) I sent Melissa a text letting her know Kyra's appointment changed. Melissa then sent multiple texts stating she needed to meet Dr. Walker, saying she “felt obliged” due to her past involvement, complaining about Life360, demanding access to anyone providing medical care for Kyra, and stating she would not pay for therapy-related involvement. This was her first request or concern since the December 18 notice.To protect Kyra’s therapy time and maintain appropriate boundaries, I emailed Dr. Walker and her office clarifying that Kyra’s individual sessions should remain focused on her care, that brief post-session check-ins were acceptable if appropriate, but any extended or parent-only consultations should be scheduled separately and billed to the requesting parent.Previously, Melissa and Alice both requested I meet with Alice after Melissa scheduled sessions for her and the kids earlier in 2025. It's my understanding Alice wanted to meet my wife and I to get a full spectrum picture and to offer up ways we could help her help Melissa rebuild her relationship with the kids. Dr. Walker was selected for Kyra’s individual therapy during my parenting time, consistent with Kyra’s request and Melissa’s prior agreement.Under our joint legal custody arrangement, medical decision-making requires notice. Notice was provided, Melissa acknowledged, and no concerns were raised until after therapy had begun.

evidence_items.title: Melissa allows school absence to extend parenting time; inconsistent explanations and gatekeeping
evidence_items.description: Melissa appears to have permitted Kyra to miss school on January 23, 2026 in part to extend parenting time, offering Kyra the benefit of staying home if she helped pack. Melissa provided conflicting explanations (severe neck/back pain vs. Kyra's later report of minor pain) and refused to discuss the absence collaboratively. Evidence suggests a pattern of school absences correlating with Melissa's work-from-home schedule. Melissa's statement to Kyra that 'your dad will use it in court' indicates awareness that the absences are problematic and constitutes coached anticipation of litigation, which may indicate parental alienation.
evidence_items.raw_text: January 23, 2026: Yesterday my wife told me that she and Kyra had talked. Kyra said she did not want to go to school the next day because of a history assignment she had not completed, which was due the following day. My wife told her she needed to go to school and encouraged her to finish the assignment before bed. Kyra said she would handle it.This morning Kyra called saying she wanted to stay home. I messaged Melissa through the app to confirm drop-off time since school was missed; she responded that drop-off would be at 5:30 p.m. I asked why Kyra was missing school. Melissa said, "Not that I owe you an explanation," and then stated that Kyra came into her room around 5:30 a.m. with a horrible kink in her neck and back, almost in tears. She said she adjusted it and used a heating pad. We briefly discussed future appointments and the need for advance notice if Kyra was going to miss school in a way that affected the parenting plan, but the conversation was not productive.Later I was on a call with Kyra and Melissa could be heard yelling in the background accusing me of harassing her and saying I was accusing her of manipulating the system to gain more time. At drop-off, Kyra said that her neck and back pain had been minor. She also told me that she had asked to stay home, offering to help Melissa pack and noting that if she did not go to school, Melissa could keep her until 5:30 p.m. Melissa agreed to this arrangement. Despite Melissa's statement that Kyra was "caught up," the history assignment that was due that day was still untouched when Kyra arrived at my home.This is consistent with prior occasions where Kyra has been allowed to stay home from school on days when Melissa is working remotely or off work. On a recent prior occasion, Melissa told Kyra she could not stay home because "your dad will use it in court." I saved the conversation in the app and documented Kyra's accounts of the events.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Not that I owe you an explanation", "if she did not go to school, Melissa could keep her until 5:30 p.m. Melissa agreed to this arrangement", "your dad will use it in court", "Kyra said that her neck and back pain had been minor", "the history assignment that was due that day was still untouched when Kyra arrived at my home"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa unilaterally took children from Eli's parents' home during his scheduled parenting time without authorization
evidence_items.description: Melissa took the children from Eli's parents' home during Eli's scheduled parenting time without his authorization, claiming it was 'effectively her weekend' because Eli was out of town. The written custody plan contains no right of first refusal provision. Additionally, Melissa unilaterally changed plans from a powwow to a show, then asserted control over the following weekend despite it being Eli's scheduled time. This pattern demonstrates gatekeeping behavior and disregard for the written schedule.
evidence_items.raw_text: January 25, 2026 – I texted Melissa to clarify Paragraph 2 of the Joint Custody Plan: Friday exchanges happen at the conclusion of school when school is in session, and at 5:30 p.m. only if school is not in session (holiday, snow day, summer), even if Kyra stays home. This led to multiple text where Melissa responded by attributing the Friday misunderstanding to my wife and then brought up 4th of July and claimed I had skipped her holiday and “stolen” a powwow weekend. She had the children on July 4, 2024, and I mistakenly believed July 4, 2025 was my year; after we began strictly following the plan in August 2025, I realized the error and told her that going forward I will follow the written holiday schedule exactly. The powwow weekend she referenced was the weekend before my August 2025 Oregon trip, which was her scheduled time. She said she planned to take Kyra to a powwow, so we returned from Duncan early so Kyra could go. Later, she changed plans and took Kyra to a show instead. The last weekend before my return was my parenting time, I planned for my parents to have the children, but while I was in Oregon Melissa went to my parents’ home and took the children without my authorization. She said it was effectively her weekend because I was out of town, even though the plan has no right of first refusal in it. All of this grew out of my attempt to clarify the Friday exchange language so we can follow the written plan going forward and avoid informal schedule changes that cause confusion and conflict.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["while I was in Oregon Melissa went to my parents' home and took the children without my authorization", "She said it was effectively her weekend because I was out of town, even though the plan has no right of first refusal in it", "The powwow weekend she referenced was the weekend before my August 2025 Oregon trip, which was her scheduled time. She said she planned to take Kyra to a powwow, so we returned from Duncan early so Kyra could go. Later, she changed plans and took Kyra to a show instead."]

evidence_items.title: Melissa denies youth group activities and church during her parenting time
evidence_items.description: During Melissa's parenting time, Melissa restricted Kyra's participation in youth group activities, required video call monitoring while Kyra was at youth group, and denied permission for Kyra to attend a youth conference and Sunday church service. Melissa cited packing/preparing to move as the reason for denying church attendance. This pattern of gatekeeping religious and extracurricular activities during Melissa's parenting time, combined with surveillance during activities, restricts Kyra's autonomy and social development.
evidence_items.raw_text: 01/31/26 – Kyra call after youth group (Melissa’s parenting time)Kyra called me during Melissa’s parenting time after attending youth group. Kyra said Melissa told her she was not comfortable with her going to youth group and had Kyra video call her and show who she was with while she was there. She said she had learned at youth group about an upcoming youth conference that she wanted to attend, but that it was scheduled on a weekend that falls during Melissa’s parenting time. Kyra said she asked Melissa if she could go to the conference and that Melissa told her no. Kyra also said she asked Melissa if she could attend regular Sunday church service, and that Melissa told her no because Kyra needed to stay home to pack and prepare to move. Kyra said she was disappointed she could not go to the youth conference or to church. I told Kyra I was sorry she could not go and that maybe next time we could look for another youth conference she could attend during my parenting time.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Kyra said Melissa told her she was not comfortable with her going to youth group and had Kyra video call her and show who she was with while she was there", "Kyra said she asked Melissa if she could go to the conference and that Melissa told her no", "Melissa told her no because Kyra needed to stay home to pack and prepare to move", "Kyra said she was disappointed she could not go to the youth conference or to church"]

evidence_items.title: Melissa falsely accuses Eli of coaching Kyra for GAL; Eli documents his actual response
evidence_items.description: Melissa sent an accusatory text claiming Eli coached Kyra on what to tell the GAL and how to manipulate her emotionally. Eli's documented response shows he advised Kyra to discuss her concerns with her therapists and the GAL independently, without scripting or coaching. This is significant because it demonstrates Melissa making unfounded allegations while Eli's actual conduct shows appropriate boundary-setting and deference to professional guidance.
evidence_items.raw_text: (Feb 28th Continued)Later that day (shortly after 5:00 p.m.), Melissa sent me a text message that said: “Seriously after hearing you prep Kyra on what to tell the GAL last night. Coaching her how to trigger me and then use my broken heart as a weapon.” This accusation that I coached Kyra on what to tell the GAL or how to cause conflict in Melissa’s home is false. When Kyra told me she did not want to go to Oklahoma City, I told her that I do not have control over Melissa’s parenting time and that she should talk with her therapists about what is going on at her mom’s home and, when the time comes, share her feelings with the GAL in her own words. I did not tell Kyra what to say or encourage her to create problems during Melissa’s time; I simply referred her to the professionals who can help her.Later that afternoon/evening, around 5:00 p.m., Kyra sent me an audio recording she had made while Melissa was yelling at her; Kyra appears to be on the phone with a friend in the background. In the recording, Kyra tries to explain that she feels uncomfortable because of the negative comments and drinking at Valerie’s, and Melissa disregards her feelings and insists they are still going.Overall, these events show that: (a) even basic co‑parenting logistics such as arranging a drop‑off are met with opposition, anger, and unanswered questions; (b) I continue to receive last‑minute notice of Kyra’s extracurricular events, limiting my ability to attend; and (c) Kyra’s preferences and comfort (including youth group, church, and not staying where she feels targeted) are not prioritized during Melissa’s parenting time. (d) Melissa’s claim that I am coaching Kyra about the GAL is false. When Kyra is upset about things that occur during Melissa’s parenting time, I simply remind her that I have no control over that time and encourage her to discuss her feelings with her therapists and, when appropriate, with the GAL, in her own words.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Seriously after hearing you prep Kyra on what to tell the GAL last night. Coaching her how to trigger me and then use my broken heart as a weapon.", "When Kyra told me she did not want to go to Oklahoma City, I told her that I do not have control over Melissa's parenting time and that she should talk with her therapists about what is going on at her mom's home and, when the time comes, share her feelings with the GAL in her own words.", "I did not tell Kyra what to say or encourage her to create problems during Melissa's time; I simply referred her to the professionals who can help her."]

evidence_items.title: Melissa refused logistics info, yelled; withheld event notice; overrode Kyra's preferences
evidence_items.description: Eli attempted routine logistics coordination on phone with Kyra; Melissa responded with anger and raised voice, refusing to answer basic questions. More significantly, Eli learned of Kyra's film festival screening only from Kyra herself at the last minute—the second such instance—preventing his attendance at a major extracurricular event. Kyra reported hearing Melissa make negative comments about her to a third party, feeling uncomfortable as a result, and being overridden when she expressed preferences to stay for religious activities, with Melissa dismissively prioritizing her own wants over Kyra's stated needs.
evidence_items.raw_text: I called Kyra on Saturday, February 28, 2026 (around 11 a.m.) to coordinate a time to drop off her clothes and other belongings at Melissa’s. While I was on the phone with Kyra, Melissa came in angry, refused to answer simple logistical questions about when/where to drop off Kyra’s things, and raised her voice. There is a 3–4 minute recording capturing me asking basic questions and Melissa becoming upset and yelling.During this same call, I learned from Kyra (not from Melissa) that her short film was being shown at a festival in Oklahoma City that weekend. This was the second time I only learned about a screening of this film at the last minute from Kyra herself (the first was the weekend of October 10, when we found out the day before an event where she was up for an award). In both situations, I did not get real advance notice or a fair opportunity to plan to attend Kyra’s extracurricular activity.Kyra told me she felt very uncomfortable spending the night at Valerie’s (Melissa’s friend’s) house because she has heard Melissa speak negatively about Kyra, and because Melissa and Valerie “just drink wine and talk crap” about me and both kids. Kyra said she feels like Valerie does not like her because the things Melissa had said. Kyra also said she wanted to stay in town for youth group on Saturday and church on Sunday instead of going to Oklahoma City. She told me that when she tried to explain this, Melissa essentially told her that it did not matter what Kyra wanted, that Melissa’s wants mattered too, and that Kyra was going. In the past, Melissa has sometimes refused to allow Kyra to attend church or church‑related activities during Melissa’s custodial time and has made demeaning comments about church and Kyra’s religious beliefs.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa came in angry, refused to answer simple logistical questions about when/where to drop off Kyra's things, and raised her voice", "I learned from Kyra (not from Melissa) that her short film was being shown at a festival in Oklahoma City that weekend", "Kyra told me she felt very uncomfortable spending the night at Valerie's (Melissa's friend's) house because she has heard Melissa speak negatively about Kyra", "Melissa and Valerie 'just drink wine and talk crap' about me and both kids", "when she tried to explain this, Melissa essentially told her that it did not matter what Kyra wanted, that Melissa's wants mattered too, and that Kyra was going"]
evidence_items.people_present: Elijah, Kyra, Melissa, Valerie (third party mentioned)

evidence_items.title: Melissa mocked father during worship, disparaged child's religious beliefs
evidence_items.description: Eli reports that Melissa publicly mocked him during church worship while Kyra was present, and over several months has repeatedly disparaged Kyra's Christian faith by calling her beliefs 'stupid,' telling her she 'only goes to church to upset' Melissa, and discouraging church attendance during her parenting time. This pattern demonstrates active undermining of the child's autonomy, beliefs, and relationship with her father, with documented emotional impact on Kyra.
evidence_items.raw_text: On Sunday, March 8, 2026, during my parenting time, Melissa came to church with us at Kyra’s request. Kyra wanted Melissa to meet people from the church and hopefully feel more comfortable so she would allow Kyra to attend an upcoming youth group lock‑in that falls on Melissa’s weekend. I told Kyra she could sit with Melissa since Melissa did not want to sit with us. After church, Kyra told me that while they were sitting together, Melissa recorded videos of me during worship, laughed and made fun of me, and made several negative remarks about me and the church.Over the past several months, Kyra has reported that during Melissa’s parenting time, Melissa has repeatedly mocked Kyra’s Christian beliefs. Melissa has told Kyra that she “only goes to church to upset” her and has called Kyra’s beliefs “stupid.” Kyra has expressed to me that it hurts her feelings when Melissa talks negatively about her chosen beliefs. Kyra has also reported that Melissa discourages her from attending church during Melissa’s parenting time, which puts more strain on their already volatile relationship. Supporting Kyra’s ability to freely participate in her chosen church and youth group activities, without being mocked or discouraged, is important to her emotional well‑being and is in her best interest. Consistently allowing Kyra to participate in important church and youth‑group events, even when they occasionally fall during the other parent’s scheduled time, supports her stability, sense of belonging, and overall best interests.
evidence_items.key_quotes: ["Melissa recorded videos of me during worship, laughed and made fun of me, and made several negative remarks about me and the church", "Melissa has repeatedly mocked Kyra's Christian beliefs", "Melissa has told Kyra that she 'only goes to church to upset' her and has called Kyra's beliefs 'stupid'", "Kyra has expressed to me that it hurts her feelings when Melissa talks negatively about her chosen beliefs", "Melissa discourages her from attending church during Melissa's parenting time"]

evidence_items.title: Eli refuses Kyra's request for summer activity; discusses logistics with co-parent
evidence_items.description: Eli refuses a request (apparently from Kyra or the co-parent) to participate in a summer community pool activity at the apartment complex. He expresses frustration about driving distance, refuses to accommodate the arrangement, dismisses the other party's concerns as illogical, and terminates the conversation abruptly despite ongoing discussion. He later discusses alternative pickup arrangements with his father but expresses irritation about having to adjust.

evidence_items.title: Eli proposes mediated discussion; Melissa resists, escalates to profanity
evidence_items.description: Eli initiates a phone call requesting a mediated discussion with a therapist before Kyra arrives for her scheduled time, citing concerns about an unspecified issue. Melissa initially resists structured conversation, then accuses Eli of guilt-tripping Kyra over an eight-month custody timeline. Melissa ultimately rejects Eli's proposed mediation structure (therapist + Eli only), insists on including her partner Aaron, and ends the call with profanity when Eli maintains his boundary.

evidence_items.title: Therapist scheduling sessions with Eli; discusses Kyra's desire to move to Duncan
evidence_items.description: Alice (therapist) calls Eli to schedule appointments. Eli confirms availability for Monday, September 29th at 5 PM in Tulsa, with follow-up family mediation session Thursday, October 2nd. Eli discusses Kyra's stated desire to move to Duncan for 9th grade then return to Union High School. Eli demonstrates flexible parenting stance, stating Kyra can change her mind at any point without court involvement. Therapist and Eli discuss Melissa's adjustment to changing parental role and agree mediation may help reduce tension.

evidence_items.title: Therapist declines to write custody letter; discusses case progress and relocation plans
evidence_items.description: Therapist Alice Coe calls Eli to decline writing a custody letter for tomorrow's court date, stating she wants to continue counseling to improve the parent-child relationship before making a professional recommendation. Alice reports initial progress in therapy (April-June) followed by deterioration after summer camp. Eli explains the relocation was already approved and they are now seeking custody modification to formalize Kyra's right to choose her living arrangement, with actual move planned for August. Discussion centers on post-relocation visitation schedules and hopes for reconciliation.

evidence_items.title: Elijah and Melissa negotiate house sale, custody schedule, and asset division

Elijah explains financial deadlines and earnest money requirements based on move-in timing. Both parties negotiate asset lists and discuss Kira's potential hybrid schooling. The conversation is substantively cooperative despite some tension regarding urgency and trust.

evidence_items.title: Melissa coordinates logistics for Kyra's belongings and immediate needs
evidence_items.description: Melissa contacts Eli to arrange logistics for Kyra's belongings. She indicates the van is full and offers options for Eli to retrieve items either immediately or later. She confirms Kyra has essential items (clothes, phone, backpack, school materials) and proposes leaving additional items in the garage. The tone is practical and cooperative.

evidence_items.title: Eli calls opposing counsel Kelly Smakel; discusses service, house agreement, relocation objection
evidence_items.description: Eli calls Melissa's attorney Kelly Smakel to obtain correct contact information after the prior attorney indicated withdrawal. The parties appear to have an agreed-upon house buyout arrangement and are working toward entry of an agreed order. However, Kelly indicates Melissa has filed an objection to Eli's relocation request and is not yet ready to agree on that matter, though Kelly suggests mediation or guardian ad litem evaluation as a path forward. Eli cooperates with service logistics and the discussion remains civil and procedurally focused.

evidence_items.description: Melissa initiated direct contact with Eli via phone call lasting over one hour on a weekday afternoon. The call duration and initiation by the opposing party suggests willingness to engage in direct communication. Without transcript or content details, this represents documented contact and potential cooperation.

Both parties escalate emotionally, with Eli refusing to participate in joint Christmas activities, citing Melissa's alleged name-slander to family and friends. Eli expresses frustration over Melissa's withholding of educational decisions (online school for Kyra) and claims Melissa has created alienation with his extended family.

evidence_items.title: Melissa refuses concert permission; Eli disputes reasoning and accuses her of dishonesty
evidence_items.description: Melissa called Eli regarding their son's (not daughter Kyra's) request to attend a concert in Oklahoma City on a school night. Melissa denied permission citing poor grades and safety concerns about the drive. Eli challenged her reasoning, arguing the grades were a pretext and the real issue was distance/control. The call deteriorated into accusations of dishonesty, with Melissa asserting parental authority and Eli demanding logical consistency in her reasoning. Melissa ended the call abruptly.

evidence_items.title: Eli and Melissa dispute holiday schedule and parental access to activities
evidence_items.description: Eli and Melissa engage in a contentious phone call regarding Kyra's holiday schedule and parental participation in her extracurricular activities (acting). Melissa refuses to release Kyra earlier than 5:30 PM on January 4th despite Eli's request, citing existing plans. Both parents invoke the parenting plan; Melissa claims both parents are entitled to attend all extracurriculars, while Eli asserts family functions are excluded. The call deteriorates into personal attacks and recriminations about past holiday disputes.
